sources.notes: Interactive task: identify the modified subarray endpoints using range-sum queries.
Official editorial compares prefix sums of the hidden permutation and the beautiful array, then uses one total-sum query to derive r after finding l.

annotation.summary: Interactively find the segment [l,r] where the hidden array differs from the permutation by +1 on that segment.
annotation.constraints: The query budget is below 40, so a logarithmic number of prefix queries is required.
annotation.core_idea: For any prefix i, the queried sum of the modified array is larger than the permutation prefix sum iff i has reached the left endpoint l. This gives a monotone predicate, so binary search l using paired prefix queries. The whole-array sum difference equals r-l+1, hence r is recovered after l is known.

solution_variants.name: interactive-prefix-binary-search
solution_variants.summary: Binary search the first prefix whose modified sum exceeds the permutation prefix, then infer the right endpoint from total sum difference.

tags.evidence: The solution is centered on choosing adaptive range-sum queries under a strict query budget.
The left endpoint is found by binary searching a monotone prefix-sum comparison.
The queried values are prefix sums, and the proof compares permutation and modified prefix sums.
